title: viola tricolor subsp. ruralis
intro: Viola tricolor subsp. ruralis, also known as Heartsease or Common Pansy, is a subspecies of the heartsease viola, a flowering plant native to Europe and Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which have been shown to help soothe skin irritations, wounds, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Viola tricolor subsp. ruralis include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestive health, and boost the immune system, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. Herbal preparations of Viola tricolor subsp. ruralis include teas, infusions, and topical creams, which can be made by infusing the flowers, leaves, or roots in hot water or a carrier oil.
